description_long: This is a large wooden half-pipe skate ramp, commonly found in urban parks or recreational areas. The structure features two vertical curved ramps on either end, connected by a flat central platform, all constructed from smooth, light-colored wood panels. Guardrails made of metal tubing are installed along the upper edges of both sides for safety, providing protection for users at the top of the ramp. The ramp's surface is seamless and continuous, designed for skateboarding, BMX biking, or scootering. The overall build is robust and sturdy, with visible support beams and reinforcements beneath the riding surface. The design is open, with no overhead covering or shade structures, emphasizing functionality and durability.
description: A large wooden half-pipe skate ramp with metal guardrails, designed for skateboarding and similar activities. It features smooth curved surfaces and a flat central platform, with a light wood finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing the vertical ramp face, guardrails, and the flat platform at the top.
description_view_1: Side perspective revealing the full curvature of the half-pipe, both platforms, and the continuous riding surface.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, with the opposite vertical ramp and guardrails visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side perspective, again showing the full curve, platforms, and guardrails.
